summary: Decomposes feature 006 into shared foundational work plus four independently validatable user-story phases: create from conversation, update/optimize user workflows, copy-then-edit builtins, and destructive user-template delete. Coverage maps every required capability operation, FR-001 through FR-015, MP-001 through MP-022, and CA-001 through CA-011 to proposed task slots.

story_phases.goal: Create a valid user workflow template from conversational intent, with guide-driven authoring, validation, direct persistence, deterministic audit results, and Settings refetch compatibility.
story_phases.independent_validation: Using an isolated CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR, guide and validate a three-phase candidate, create it through workflow_template_authoring, verify persisted:true/source:user, verify invalid candidates do not write, and verify /api/workflows/templates or Settings refetch can list the created template.

proposed_tasks.title: Implement guide/list/validate/create service behavior for conversational create
Expose create-capable workflow_template_authoring tool contract and rendering
Add create-path API parity and same-store visibility tests

story_phases.goal: Optimize an existing user workflow by inspecting a fresh basis, validating the edited template, preserving compatible unknown fields, and rejecting stale updates without writing.
story_phases.independent_validation: Inspect a user template to capture basisHash, update it with a matching selector/id and fresh hash, verify success and unknown-field preservation, then simulate a manual edit and verify the old basisHash rejects with persisted:false and WORKFLOW_TEMPLATE_STALE_BASIS.

proposed_tasks.title: Add inspect and basisHash support for current user-template editing
Implement user-template update with validation, id/source checks, stale-write rejection, and unknown-field preservation
Cover update success, stale rejection, builtin-update rejection, and config-unchanged failures

story_phases.goal: Modify builtin workflows safely by rejecting direct builtin mutation, duplicating the builtin to a non-conflicting user-owned template, and applying future edits only to the copy.
story_phases.independent_validation: Request a change to builtin agent-development, verify direct update is rejected with copy-builtin-first guidance, duplicate creates agent-development-custom or suffixed alternatives, verify the builtin is unchanged, and verify subsequent update targets the user copy.

proposed_tasks.title: Implement duplicate/copy behavior with builtin copy naming and conflict handling
Wire duplicate operation through tool and local authoring API
Test builtin copy-then-edit behavior, default naming, suffixes, and builtin immutability

story_phases.goal: Delete only a uniquely identified user workflow template with destructive metadata, basisHash freshness, target-source protection, and refreshed list metadata.
story_phases.independent_validation: Inspect a user template to capture basisHash, delete with source:user/id/hash and verify destructive metadata plus refreshed list output, then verify builtin, missing, ambiguous, and stale delete attempts reject without writing.

proposed_tasks.title: Implement destructive user-template delete with target and stale checks
Expose delete metadata, permission classification, and transcript audit rendering
Test delete success and no-write rejections for builtin, ambiguous, stale, missing, and invalid config targets

foundational_tasks.title: Extract or centralize workflow template validation shared by registry, API, and authoring service
Create deterministic field guide and alignment tests for required fields, allowed values, unsupported shapes, and repair hints
Create WorkflowTemplateAuthoringService result envelope, operation dispatch, no-write failure model, and registry-only persistence boundary
Add local desktop/server authoring endpoint for cache-owning process writes and tool fallback failure handling
Add global tool registration and operation-level metadata for read-only, mutating, destructive, and non-concurrency-safe behavior
Integrate active workflow phase-policy gating for mutating authoring operations while preserving read-only access
Finalize verification lanes and evidence collection for server/tool/API/policy coverage and optional desktop checks

stop_and_reopen_conditions: Implementation cannot keep direct validated create/update persistence and instead requires mandatory preview-only flow.
Any task proposes direct builtin mutation, builtin deletion, or user-template shadowing of builtin ids.
Workflow authoring writes would need to mutate protected non-workflow state or bypass WorkflowTemplateRegistryService.
Desktop-launched authoring cannot preserve registry/API semantics through either the planned local server endpoint or an explicitly documented equivalent.
Field guide content cannot be deterministically tested against validation-required fields, allowed values, unsupported shapes, and repair concepts.
Active or historical workflow session snapshots would need in-place mutation for this feature to work.
Unsupported parallel, nested, branching, or loop workflow shapes become required for acceptance.
risks: Validation extraction can accidentally change existing manual API behavior; T001 and T013 should require parity tests before story work is accepted.
Desktop server path can fail open if the tool silently falls back to direct file writes; T004 must assert visible no-write failure when CC_JIANGXIA_DESKTOP_SERVER_URL is configured and unavailable.
Operation-level policy can over-block read-only guide/list/inspect/validate if implemented as whole-tool denial; T006 should test read-only allowed and mutations denied.
Unknown-field preservation depends on using the registry writer and mapping updates carefully; T022/T023 should include old-fixture style cases.
Delete target resolution and stale checks are safety-sensitive; T041/T043 should be ordered after inspect/basisHash support is stable.
notes_for_leader: Recommended task ordering: T001-T006 foundation first, then US1 create, US2 update, US3 duplicate, US4 delete, with T007 final verification/evidence consolidation.
T004 is foundational because desktop cache ownership affects every mutating operation, but desktop frontend files should remain optional unless implementation proves UI/client changes are required.
The proposed IDs are stable for lane discussion; the leader may renumber when integrating task packets.
Do not place all tests at the end. Each story should include same-area tests with no-write assertions before moving to the next mutating capability.

A single operation-discriminated tool preserves the capability map while minimizing tool surface; task generation should avoid splitting guide/list/inspect/validate/create/update/duplicate/delete into unrelated tool names.
